displayName: Moon â€” Shackleton crater rim (lunar south pole)
sourceProduct: LOLA South Pole DEM Mosaic 5 m/px (ldem_87s_5mpp), NASA GSFC Planetary Geodesy Data Archive product 81
credit: NASA LRO LOLA / NASA GSFC Planetary Geodesy Data Archive. Cite: Barker, M.K., et al. (2021), Planetary and Space Science 203, 105119, doi:10.1016/j.pss.2020.105119
notes: 20x20 km polar-stereographic window containing the lunar south pole (which sits on Shackleton's western rim crest) and the entire ~21 km crater bowl; 4.44 km rim-to-floor relief. Laser altimetry, so the permanently shadowed floor is real measured data (cameras cannot see it). 100% valid pixels, no interpolation. Elevations are metres above the 1737.4 km lunar reference sphere.
